_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to explain why changes happened and what the results of those changes were.

_source.w: We are learning to explain why changes happened and what the results of those changes were.
y: Understanding causes and consequences helps us see that historical events do not happen by accident.
Recognising consequences — both good and bad — helps us learn from the past.
This skill helps us think about how choices today will shape the future.
eg: Drawing a cause-and-consequence diagram for a key event in early colonisation.
Describing how migration changed a local community and what the effects were.
Using a fishbone diagram to explore the multiple causes of a historical event.
Discussing the consequences of colonisation for Aboriginal Peoples and for British settlers.
Investigating why a significant local change happened and who it affected most.
Writing a paragraph that explains 'This happened because...' and 'As a result...'
